source.conversation_context: Narada task assignment sequencing vs immediate assignability

title: Do not present dependency-blocked task order as immediately assignable roster work

content.principle: When recommending multiple task assignments, distinguish explicitly between what is assignable now and what is only a future dependency-ordered sequence. Never format blocked future work as if it can be assigned immediately.
content.rationale: Collapsing planned order and present assignability causes operator-facing assignment errors and misstates what the CLI/DAG will accept. Narada task governance requires dependency truth to remain explicit at recommendation time, not only at command failure time.
content.contract_patch: Add assignment guidance: separate 'Now assignable' from 'Queued after dependency closure' whenever any recommended task is currently blocked by the DAG.
not_applicable_when: All recommended tasks are immediately claimable
The operator explicitly asked only for a long-range sequence and not for current assignments
The response clearly labels the output as a dependency-ordered plan rather than immediate assignment
